items.title: 'Let them shower in hotels': Johannesburg Premier faces backlash amid water crisis
items.description: In South Africa, as taps run dry in Johannesburg, Africa's richest city, a tone deaf remark by a senior politician there unleashes fury.

items.title: Greetings from Cairo, where lights and decorations transform the city during Ramadan
items.description: As Ramadan begins, traditional lanterns called fawanees brighten Cairo. They have become a symbol of Ramadan and are an almost-mandatory home decorati...

items.title: Sweet redemption for Mikaela Shiffrin, who wins Olympic gold
items.description: Third race is the charm for Shiffrin, who won gold today after failing to podium in her first two races of the 2026 Olympic Games.

items.title: Mark Zuckerberg faces a jury today. And, the top DHS spokesperson resigns her post
items.description: Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand today in a trial over whether social media companies are fueling the teen mental health crisis. And, Tricia McLaughlin...

items.title: Ogden, Schumacher grab silver for U.S. in Olympic cross-country team sprint, Diggins falls short
items.description: U.S. cross-country skiers Ben Ogden and Gus Schumacher power to a silver medal in the men's team sprint. U.S. women led by Jessie Diggins finish off t...
